summary: The answer is the maximum decimal digit, and an adversary may place the unique maximum at any uninspected position; one full character scan matches the resulting linear lower bound.

optimality.argument: Consider two length-L inputs that agree everywhere already inspected, with all remaining digits one in the first input and a nine at the last uninspected position in the second. Their required answers differ, so a worst-case exact algorithm must inspect every position. Tracking the maximum during one scan takes O(L) time.

replacement_checks.evidence: The unique answer-determining maximum can occur at any position, so skipping one character cannot distinguish otherwise identical inputs with different answers.
A single left-to-right scan converts each digit and updates one running maximum, using constant auxiliary storage.
Authored fixtures cover all-one deci-binary inputs, zero digits, uniform digits, an internal maximum, digit nine, a far-right maximum, and a long decimal string.
